name: Texas Barbequed Beef Brisket
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the external fat on the beef brisket to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
2. Combine 2 teaspoons of Paprika and half a teaspoon of ground black pepper; rub the mixture evenly over the surface of the beef brisket.
3. Place the brisket, with the fat side down, in an 11 1/2 x 9-inch disposable foil pan. Add 1 cup of water.
4. Cover the pan tightly with aluminum foil and place it in the center of the grill over very low coals (use a single layer of coals with space between each). Cover the grill.
5. Cook for 5 to 6 hours, turning the brisket over every 1 1/2 hours. Use a baster to remove accumulated fat from the pan.
6. If needed during cooking, add up to half a cup of water to the pan.
7. Remove the brisket and place it on the grill, fat side down, directly over very low coals. Reserve the pan drippings and cover; continue cooking for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
8. Meanwhile, skim the fat from the pan drippings and reserve 1 cup of drippings. Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add the grated onion and cook until it becomes tender crisp.
9. Add the reserved pan drippings, remaining half teaspoon of ground black pepper, catsup, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, and hot pepper sauce to the saucepan. Simmer for 15 minutes.
10. Carve the brisket into thin slices across the grain. Serve with the sauce, garnished with fresh peppers and lemon and lime slices.
Note: For a smokier flavor, soak oak, pecan, mesquite, or hickory chips in water for 30 minutes before adding them to the very low coals.
